item.title: Franna Crane Project
item.description: A Franna crane project involves fabrication, machining, and repair work in the workshop. What challenges come with maintaining heavy lifting equipment?

media:content.media:title: Franna Crane Project

item.title: Oxy gas cutting on a D11 ripper shank
item.description: Oxy gas cutting techniques are used on a D11 ripper shank during heavy repair and fabrication work. Why is this method effective for thick steel?

media:content.media:title: Oxy gas cutting on a D11 ripper shank

item.title: Building a New Hydraulic Rod
item.description: A new rod is machined and assembled with precision for heavy equipment applications. What steps ensure strength and accuracy in the final build?

media:content.media:description: A new rod is machined and assembled with precision for heavy equipment applications. What steps ensure strength and accuracy in the final build?

item.title: What Is Air Arc Gouging?
item.description: A look at the air arc gouging process and how it’s used to remove metal in heavy fabrication and repair work. What makes this technique useful in workshops?

media:content.media:title: What Is Air Arc Gouging?
media:content.media:description: A look at the air arc gouging process and how it’s used to remove metal in heavy fabrication and repair work. What makes this technique useful in workshops?

item.title: Major overhaul for a stick cylinder
item.description: A stick cylinder receives a full overhaul involving repair, machining, and rebuilding for reliable performance. What’s involved in restoring hydraulic components?

item.description: Heavy restoration work brings an excavator arm and bucket back into working condition. What techniques help extend the life of equipment like this?

media:content.media:description: Heavy restoration work brings an excavator arm and bucket back into working condition. What techniques help extend the life of equipment like this?

item.title: Crack Testing an Industrial Spindle
item.description: A spindle undergoes crack testing to inspect for wear and structural issues in a workshop setting. Why is early detection so important in heavy machining?
